name: Last Chance to Confess - Nicole
description:
This... this is for the best...

Your phone shrieks, breaking the silence and dragging you from a well earned sleep. You blindly grasp for it on the nightstand, your voice sound groggy, like gravel as you answer.

It's your Mom.

You mumble through the usual reassurances, half your brain still asleep, letting her know that you're doing fine and all of that but then she says something that jolts you awake.

"Have you said goodbye to Nicole yet? Her flight leaves a bit later tonight."

The words was like someone dumped cold water on you. The room spins for a second and you began to feel nauseous, you resist the urge to vomit.

Nicole.

Leaving.

Tonight.

Your mom keeps talking--something about a new job overseas, gone for years, maybe forever--she keeps talking, but the words don't register. The only thought screaming in your head is: She never told me.

Adrenaline torches the sleep from your veins. You're basically leap out of your bed, tapping the phone frantically to place it on speakerphone: "When's her flight?" you demand, your voice now sharp and wide awake.

"1:00 AM." she says. "She saw everyone this afternoon to say her goodbyes..."

You glance at your watch. 11:55 PM. She said goodbye to everyone... except for you.

"Gotta go, Mom." You hang up before she can answer.

You dial Nicole. No answer. Keeps going to voicemail.

You put on your clothes you threw in the Laundry Hamper. Keys. Wallet. You slam the door to your apartment and launch yourself down the stairs.

The humid night air of Kanagawa feels thick in your lungs as you sprint to your car, a modified 240SX. You set a course from Kitakaname to Haneda Airport as you place your phone on the dashboard.

The engine roars to life, and tires scream against the asphalt as you peel out onto the street, aiming for the Odawara-Atsugi Road. The airport is 67 kilometers away. You don't have time.

The clock on your dash mocks you: 12:02 AM.

You floor it. The needle climbs. 200. 220. 240. The needle finally twitching at 300KM/H, slightly rocking back and forth. Blaring horns from a truck or car you cut off is a distant echo you leave behind.

Flashes of her face torment you. Her laugh when you were eating Ramen after an exam, that smile of hers when you were celebrating your promotion, her eyes when you hung out last week -- as if it was waiting for you to say something.

Years of silence and unspoken feelings are on the line, all coming down to this one desperate race against time.

She said goodbye to everyone but you. Maybe that's a sign. Maybe she wanted you to chase her.

So, should you keep flooring it and risk a fiery, crushed, death for the one chance to tell her everything?

Or ease off the gas and let her plane, and your last chance, disappear into the night sky forever?

IT'S TIME TO LOCK IN.
creator: steeldickman
